data.content:
Plan:
1. Add overview-specific helpers in `src/lib/beat-state-overview.ts`: required state columns, hidden states (`shipped`), active-state detection, full/short overview labels, and grouping that emits empty required columns while preserving unknown/extra states when beats exist.
2. Update `src/components/beat-state-overview.tsx` to render empty state columns, suppress shipped, use full ids/aliases only when All Repositories is selected, and add a compact lease metadata line for active-state tiles. Lease age will come from the active step start time or active terminal start time; provider/model/version will come from canonical lease agent info when present.
3. Update `src/app/beats/page.tsx` to pass an explicit All Repositories flag and terminal-derived lease info into the overview. Keep the existing lightbox-only click behavior and no drag/drop affordances.
4. Extend `src/lib/__tests__/beat-state-overview.test.ts` for shipped filtering, required empty columns, full-vs-short labels, active-state detection, and lease metadata extraction.
5. Run `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, `bun run build`, then verify `/beats?view=overview&state=queued` in desktop/mobile browser.

Complexity: medium-small UI/data-shaping pass.
Risks: active lease metadata is only displayable when available in beat metadata or active terminal state; the UI should omit missing fields rather than fabricating placeholders. Empty columns increase horizontal width, so overflow must remain contained inside the overview rail.
